contribution_description:
[CONTRIBUTION EVENT]
- Type: Time (Minutes)
- Amount: 30
- Description: Diagnose Beer Hall daily digest failure + document the automated pipeline (flow diagram + troubleshooting)
  
  Pull requests (GitHub evidence):
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/agentic_ai_context/pull/117
  
  Details:
  Gary noticed today's Beer Hall digest hadn't been generated on TrueSightDAO/ecosystem_change_logs. Diagnosed the failure and closed the documentation gap that made the diagnosis slow.
  
  Diagnosis:
  - Pulled the most recent runs of market_research/.github/workflows/beer-hall-digest-daily.yml via gh CLI. The 9 prior days (2026-05-02 through 2026-05-10) all succeeded; today's 02:49 UTC run FAILED.
  - Drilled into the failed step: 'Draft Message 1 + Message 2 via Claude'. All downstream steps (Archive digest, Refresh advisory snapshot, Push branches, Open and merge PRs) showed SKIPPED.
  - Grepped the run log for the actual error: anthropic.BadRequestError: 400 — 'Your credit balance is too low to access the Anthropic API'. Account had hit zero between yesterday's successful run and today's run; no rate-limit or auth issue.
  - Reported back with the exact fix (top up at console.anthropic.com/settings/billing for the account that owns the ANTHROPIC_API_KEY secret on go_to_market) + manual re-trigger command (gh workflow run beer-hall-digest-daily.yml -R TrueSightDAO/go_to_market).
  
  Quick exploratory loop on model alternatives Gary asked about — DeepSeek (~30x cheaper, already in DAO infra via truesight_autopilot), Grok (less established for structured summarization), bigmodel.cn / Zhipu GLM-4 (cost-competitive but no DAO precedent, China-hosted API reachability from US GitHub Actions runners, English prose less battle-tested). Recommendation: top up Claude (~/day for this workflow) + add a credit-balance pre-flight; consolidation onto DeepSeek is a separate 'one LLM provider for ops' decision, not a fix for today's outage.
  
  Pipeline mapping — confirmed the three-script chain:
    1. market_research/scripts/generate_beer_hall_preview.py — walks git log + merged PRs + Telegram chat logs + DApp Remarks across 11 sibling DAO repos to produce the evidence preview
    2. market_research/scripts/draft_beer_hall_digest.py — Anthropic API (Claude Sonnet 4.6) drafts the two WhatsApp-format messages (this is the step that failed today; not previously named in agentic_ai_context)
    3. ecosystem_change_logs/scripts/archive_beer_hall_changelog.py — writes the per-day entry (.md + .json) and rebuilds the static feed pagination (manifest.json + page-N.json)
  Plus market_research/scripts/generate_advisory_snapshot.py refreshes the oracle context corpus in parallel.
  
  agentic_ai_context PR #117 (merged): new 'Daily Beer Hall pipeline — automated archive flow' subsection in OPENCLAW_WHATSAPP.md.
  - Mermaid flow diagram (renders natively on GitHub) — cron 00:00 UTC -> sibling checkouts -> preview -> LLM-draft (highlighted in red as the most-common failure point) -> archive -> advisory snapshot -> PR + auto-merge on both target repos.
  - Per-script summary table: Step / Script / Repo / Purpose / Most likely failure for all five scripts including draft_beer_hall_digest.py which had been undocumented despite being where every cost-driven failure manifests.
  - Troubleshooting section: 'today's digest didn't appear' with copy-pasteable gh run list / gh run view / log-grep commands + a mapping table from common log-error strings (credit balance too low, 401 invalid x-api-key, 429 rate limit, gh pr create 403, no run at all = workflow auto-disabled after 60 days inactivity) to the corresponding fix.
  
  Net effect: next time the daily digest is missing, the diagnostic loop becomes 'open OPENCLAW_WHATSAPP.md -> scan troubleshooting table -> match error -> apply fix' instead of 'read workflow YAML, infer script names, guess failure mode'. Cuts diagnosis time substantially for any operator (human or AI) hitting this again.
- Contributor(s): Gary Teh
- TDG Issued: 50.00
- Attached Filename: N/A
- Destination Contribution File Location: N/A
--------
